evals.prompt: I need to upgrade my .NET for Android BarcodeCapture app from Scandit SDK v6 to v7. Here is my current Activity: ActivityV6.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill replaces the manual CameraSettings/VideoResolution.Auto block with BarcodeCapture.RecommendedCameraSettings, informs the user about the new default ScanIntention.Smart, and notes the composite-codes default change. It does NOT rename BarcodeCapture.Create — the .NET factory is unchanged.

assertions.text: The CameraSettings with VideoResolution.Auto block is removed
The user is informed about the new default ScanIntention.Smart in v7
The migration guide URL (https://docs.scandit.com/sdks/net/android/migrate-6-to-7/) is provided
BarcodeCapture.Create( is preserved (not renamed to something else)
The IBarcodeCaptureListener implementation (OnBarcodeScanned, etc.) is preserved

evals.prompt: I'm upgrading my Scandit .NET Android barcode scanning SDK from v7 to v8. What do I need to change in my code?
evals.expected_output: The skill explains that .NET Android BarcodeCapture has no breaking factory-method changes in v7→v8. Mentions the formal VideoResolution.Auto deprecation, and that BarcodeCaptureLicenseInfo is available from 8.4 and SelectionMode from 8.5.

assertions.text: The skill mentions there are no breaking factory-method changes for .NET Android BarcodeCapture in v7→v8
The skill does NOT instruct the user to replace BarcodeCapture.Create with new BarcodeCapture(settings) plus AddMode (that is a cross-platform SDK change, not .NET Android)
